sections.paragraph:
γ´ numerical sign = 3 (τρεῖς: pap; Jos., C. Ap. 1, 157; cp. SibOr 5:24; Ar. [JTS 25, ’24, 76, ln. 28; Mel., P. 90 B]) or 3d (τρίτη) in the superscr. of 3J (sc. ἐπιστολή), of Hv 3 (ὅρασις); Hm 3 (ἐντολή); Hs 3 (παραβολή). S. also TestSol; PVindobBosw 18, 37 superscr. (δεκατός).

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

JTS JTS = Journal of Theological Studies—List 6

  1. ln. = line

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

superscr. superscr. = superscription

  1. sc. = scilicet (one may understand, supply)

Hv Hv = Visions

Hm Hm = Mandates

Hs Hs = Similitudes

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

PVindobBosw PVindobBosw = Einige Wiener Papyri, ed. EBoswinkel—List 4

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 185
